html:
DRDGOLD Thesis

DRDGOLD is no longer the hidden sub-$100 million oddity implied by bad data feeds. At roughly 4,449c per JSE share, the real market cap is about R38.5 billion, not USD 22.9 million. The NYSE ADR represents 10 ordinary shares, so the ADR and JSE lines reconcile once the ratio and USD/ZAR are handled correctly. The stock is not a 1x free cash flow fantasy. It is better described as a debt-free, unhedged, surface-tailings gold compounder trading around 7 to 9x current-cycle earnings while funding a major production and life-extension buildout internally.

The variant view is that DRDGOLD is not just a finite mine-dump processor. It is becoming a long-life mine-waste infrastructure platform. FY2025 reserves were 5.85Moz, Vision 2028 targets 3.0Mt per month of throughput and 6 tonnes of annual gold output, and the current capital cycle is building the deposition, pipeline, plant, solar and storage backbone needed to convert that resource base into a longer-duration cash stream. The 2025 integrated report frames Vision 2028 as an R8 billion program and explicitly targets 6 tonnes of gold annually by FY2028. That is materially above FY2026 guidance of 140,000 to 150,000 ounces.

The latest operating evidence is strong. In the March 2026 quarter, DRDGOLD produced 39,192oz, revenue rose to R2.96 billion, adjusted EBITDA reached R1.81 billion, AISC was about $2,034/oz, and cash increased to R2.32 billion even after paying the March interim dividend. The company remained free of bank debt and said it was on track for the upper end of FY2026 production guidance. The important point is that the quarter was not a stale trailing multiple story: it was a current gold price story, with realized gold at $4,886/oz and spot gold still near $4,545/oz on May 18, 2026.

The H1 FY2026 result showed the same inflection earlier in the year: revenue of R5.05 billion, profit of R1.93 billion, EPS of 222.7c, operating cash flow of R2.31 billion, R1.65 billion of capex funded from operating cash flow, and a 50c interim dividend. Management also highlighted the transfer of the Kloof 2 dump from Sibanye-Stillwater to FWGR, adding about 67Mt of mineral resource, and noted that future Sibanye tailings transfers remain part of the original FWGR framework.

This is the real upside engine: DRDGOLD is using a high rand gold price to buy duration. The market sees a gold miner with high capex. The better read is that today’s gold price is funding tomorrow’s throughput, deposition capacity and life extension without balance sheet stress. Net cash is not enough by itself to make this cheap, but DRDGOLD’s cash is productive because it is financing Vision 2028 while still supporting dividends. A lazy net-cash screen misses the point; the question is what earnings look like after DP2, RTSF, Daggafontein and Withok are online.

On current-cycle math, DRDGOLD can plausibly earn R4.4 billion to R4.8 billion in FY2026, or roughly 500c to 550c per share. That puts the stock around 8x to 9x FY2026 earnings at R44.49. FY2027 and FY2028 are more interesting: if production rises toward 170,000 to 193,000oz and cash costs stay near R1.0 million to R1.1 million/kg, earnings power at a $4,500 gold price can move toward 650c to 750c per share. If gold averages closer to $5,000 and the rand does not sharply strengthen, EPS can move above 800c.

That is not “too late because the stock ran.” The stock moved because the earnings power changed. FY2025 headline EPS was 260.6c; H1 FY2026 alone nearly matched that. The forward denominator has changed faster than the share price.

The misunderstood complexity is operational, not geological. DRDGOLD has no deep-level mining risk in the usual South African sense. It hydraulically reclaims tailings, pumps slurry, processes low-grade material at scale, and deposits residue safely. That makes permitting, deposition capacity, water, power stability, community relations and tailings integrity the real value drivers. The solar and battery system matters because Ergo’s electricity cost fell despite Eskom tariff pressure. The five-year Ergo wage agreement also reduces a near-term labor overhang.

Retail context is not euphoric. StockTwits shows no useful DRD sentiment score, and recent JSE_Bets discussion around the May operating update was small and cautious, noting good EBITDA growth but also lower gold sold. The crowd is broadly talking gold, Harmony and ETFs, not underwriting DRDGOLD’s Vision 2028 transition. That supports the idea that this is still a fundamentals rerating rather than a meme unwind.

The bear case is straightforward: gold falls hard, the rand strengthens, Withok or RTSF timelines slip, and growth capex consumes more cash than expected. Tailings failures are low-probability but existential. Sibanye’s 50.1% control is double-edged: it supplies tailings optionality and could support consolidation, but minorities remain dependent on fair related-party behavior. The stock is not protected from a gold drawdown just because the balance sheet is clean.

Still, the setup is attractive because the base case does not require heroic multiples. A debt-free gold producer with 19 consecutive years of dividends, a self-funded expansion path, and visible FY2028 production growth should not trade below 7x normalized forward earnings if current gold prices persist.

Targets

Current price used: 4,449c, or R44.49 per JSE share.

Bear case: R30, down about 33%. Assumes gold falls toward $3,200/oz, FY2027 EPS normalizes near 300c, capex overruns, and the market applies a subdued 9 to 10x multiple.

Base case: R72, up about 62%. Assumes gold around $4,300 to $4,600/oz, FY2027 to FY2028 EPS of 650c to 750c, successful DP2/RTSF progress, and a still-conservative 10x multiple.

Bull case: R115, up about 158%. Assumes gold averages $5,000+, Vision 2028 reaches roughly 6 tonnes of annual output, growth capex rolls off, dividends step up, and the market values DRDGOLD at 12 to 13x 850c to 950c of earnings power.
